2018-08-25.title: Pope in Ireland: Francis Meets Church Abuse Victims
2018-08-25.abstract: Abuse survivors and their advocates around the world were closely watching what the pope said about the crisis that has undermined the church around the world.

2018-08-25.title: In Ireland, Pope Francis Finds a Country Transformed and a Church in Tatters
2018-08-25.abstract: Many Catholics were hoping Francis would use the wreckage of the Irish church as a backdrop to unveil muscular measures to protect children from abuse. On the first day of his visit, he disappointed.

2018-08-26.title: In Anarchic Corner of Copenhagen, Police and Dealers Play Cat and Mouse
2018-08-26.abstract: Danish authorities tolerate Freetown Christiania, a long-running utopian experiment with an open-air drug market. But tolerance has its limits, and the mood in Denmark is tilting toward law-and-order.

2018-08-26.title: Residents: 12. Average Age: 75. Biggest Challenge: Avoiding Extinction.
2018-08-26.abstract: In Corippo, Switzerland, the average age is 75, and the village has far more abandoned homes, 30, than residents, 12. But officials have a grand plan to fill those empty houses with tourists.

2018-08-26.title: Pope Francis in Ireland: Call for Resignation Further Clouds Visit
2018-08-26.abstract: A former top Vatican official asserted that the pope had known of abuses by a disgraced U.S. prelate and called on Francis to resign.

2018-08-26.title: Police in Turkey Break Up Mothers’ Weekly Vigil
2018-08-26.abstract: Officers used tear gas to stop a regular demonstration to remember people thought to have disappeared in police custody. Nearly 50 people were arrested.

2018-08-26.title: Australia’s New Prime Minister, Scott Morrison, Appoints a Cabinet
2018-08-26.abstract: After a week of political chaos, the most significant changes involved foreign affairs, immigration and energy.

2018-08-27.title: North Korea to Expel Japanese Tourist Accused of Crime
2018-08-27.abstract: North Korea says it has decided to “leniently condone” the man and expel him “on the principle of humanitarianism.”

2018-08-27.title: In Russia, a Top University Lacks Just One Thing: Students
2018-08-27.abstract: The European University at St. Petersburg lost its teaching license after attacks from conservative nationalists who resent its Western orientation.

2018-08-27.title: Scott Morrison Is a New Kind of Australian Prime Minister: An Evangelical Christian
2018-08-27.abstract: Sworn in last week, the new leader has talked openly and often about his evangelical Christian faith — a rarity in Australian politics.

2018-08-27.title: Myanmar Generals Should Face Genocide Charges Over Rohingya, U.N. Says
2018-08-27.abstract: A United Nations panel says that Myanmar’s top general, Min Aung Hlaing, should be among those tried for a “plan for destruction” of Rohingya Muslims.

2018-08-27.title: Ally of South Korean Leader Conspired to Rig Online Opinion, Inquiry Finds
2018-08-27.abstract: A special counsel found that a key ally of President Moon of South Korea conspired in an illegal attempt to influence public opinion ahead of Mr. Moon’s election.

2018-08-28.title: Vatican Power Struggle Bursts Into Open as Conservatives Pounce
2018-08-28.abstract: Vatican intrigues usually remain behind the walls. The battle now is fueled by a potent combination of conservative anger and the child sex abuse scandals.

2018-08-28.title: Australia Sends Migrants Found in Crocodile-Infested Waters to Detention
2018-08-28.abstract: The government said Tuesday that the 17 migrants would be sent to Christmas Island, a remote Australian territory, where their status would be determined.

2018-08-28.title: Duterte Is Accused of Murder in New Filing at Hague Court
2018-08-28.abstract: Relatives of eight Filipinos killed in President Rodrigo Duterte’s drug war filed the new complaint against him at the International Criminal Court.

2018-08-28.title: German Far Right and Counterprotesters Clash in Chemnitz
2018-08-28.abstract: Demonstrations in the city began after a 35-year-old man was killed over the weekend, and nationalists called on people to “defend” their country from immigrants.

2018-08-28.title: Iran’s Parliament Gives President a Rare Rebuke
2018-08-28.abstract: Lawmakers asked Hassan Rouhani to explain an economic crisis that has worsened as a result of U.S. sanctions, and then voted to reject his explanations.

2018-08-29.title: The Man Who Took On Pope Francis: The Story Behind the Viganò Letter
2018-08-29.abstract: Archbishop Carlo Maria Viganò wrote a 7,000-word letter that called for the resignation of Pope Francis, accusing him of covering up sexual abuse in the Roman Catholic Church.

2018-08-29.title: With Ships and Missiles, China Is Ready to Challenge U.S. Navy in Pacific
2018-08-29.abstract: China’s Navy is now the world’s largest. It has altered the military balance in Asia in ways the U.S. is only beginning to digest.

2018-08-29.title: Hong Kong Professor Arrested After Wife’s Body Is Found in Suitcase
2018-08-29.abstract: The case comes as another professor in Hong Kong faces a high-profile trial in the deaths of his wife and daughter involving a gas-filled yoga ball.

2018-08-29.title: Pollution May Dim Thinking Skills, Study in China Suggests
2018-08-29.abstract: The impact of air pollution appeared especially stark in older men, a particularly worrying sign for countries with aging populations.

2018-08-29.title: Posters Suggesting That Women Can Drink While Pregnant Stir Backlash
2018-08-29.abstract: The posters, distributed throughout Australia, were made by a group largely funded by the alcohol industry, and their wording was changed after an outcry.

2018-08-30.title: For Foreigners Imprisoned by Iran, an Uncertain Plight
2018-08-30.abstract: There was a flicker of hope when Iran briefly released Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe, a Briton, from a prison. Now she and other foreigners held there can only wait.

2018-08-30.title: ‘Beach With No Water’ Draws Sneers in Rome. But It Has Fans, Too.
2018-08-30.abstract: A riverfront beach has been criticized as too little, too late for this summer, but for those who cannot get to the seaside, it’s a welcome option.

2018-08-30.title: Australia Plans to Deny Chelsea Manning an Entry Visa, Citing ‘Criminal Record’
2018-08-30.abstract: The Australian government said Ms. Manning, who was convicted of sharing classified American military documents, failed a character test.

2018-08-30.title: Maureen Dowd, in Australia, Explains Trump’s ‘Ego Arithmetic’
2018-08-30.abstract: Our opinion columnist is in Australia, sharing her blistering take on politics and the media in America.

2018-08-30.title: For Sale: 55-Foot-Tall Lobster. Owners in a Pinch. Can You Help?
2018-08-30.abstract: A giant lobster named Larry is at the center of a small town’s identity. What happens if he leaves? And what does a dispute about its future say about the culture of Australia’s “Big Things”?

2018-08-31.title: How Germany’s Far Right Stole a Rallying Cry for Democracy
2018-08-31.abstract: Violent demonstrations against refugees have featured a chant that translates to English as “we are the people.”

2018-08-31.title: Chemnitz Protests Show New Strength of Germany’s Far Right
2018-08-31.abstract: A rampage in the eastern city now stands as a high-water mark in the outpouring of anti-immigrant hatred that has swelled in Germany.

2018-08-31.title: Hacking a Prince, an Emir and a Journalist to Impress a Client
2018-08-31.abstract: The NSO Group, a spyware maker, targeted the phones of powerful figures to show off its product, emails show. It’s now accused of illegal spying in lawsuits.

2018-08-31.title: Australia’s Catholic Leaders Reject Call to Report Sex Abuse Heard in Confessions
2018-08-31.abstract: Catholic officials in Australia said the proposal would violate a sacred rite, infringe on religious freedom and do little to protect children from predatory priests.

2018-08-31.title: China’s State Media: Trump Tweets Come From an ‘Alternative Universe’
2018-08-31.abstract: In recent days, China’s English-language state media outlets have grown more strident in their mockery of the president and his policies.
